lines.en_lit: “To that within the veil; and you shall serve; within the veil, that there is services of giving but not services of removing This outside even a service of removal,

If so And you shall serve [va’avadtem],” also to what is within the veil. a complete service and not a service that has another service after it But outside even a service that has a service after it.
And you shall serve [va’avadtem],” the verse goes back and combines
Rava raised a dilemma What a service of removal in the Sanctuary we compare to inside or we compare to the outer
went back and resolved Within And to what is within
However, if so a non-priest who arranges the table should be liable. there is arranging of the vessels a non-priest who arranges the vessels should be liable. there is removal and the burning
a non-priest who arranges the candelabrum should be liable. There is placing of the wicks placed a wick should be liable. there is placing the oil.
placed the oil should be liable. there is lighting who lit should be liable. Lighting is not service,

And is not But wasn’t it taught “And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar and lay out wood in order upon the fire” teaches that the kindling of chips [alita] be performed only by a proper priest and the priestly garments? Kindling chips is a service, lighting not service.
However, if so a non-priest who set up the arrangement should be liable, There is the arrangement of two logs arranged the two logs should be liable. There is the arrangement of limbs

But didn’t Rav Asi say Rabbi Yoḥanan said: A non-priest who arranged the two pieces of wood is liable? disagree on this One Sage, holds is a service that is complete and one Sage, holds is not a service that is complete

It was taught in accordance with Rav, it was taught in accordance with Levi. It was taught in accordance with Rav: services for which a non-priest is liable to death the sprinkling of blood, whether inside or inside the innermost and one who sprinkles bird sin-offering; and one who squeezes a bird burnt-offering or burns and one who pours out three log of water or three log of wine

It was taught in accordance with Levi: services for which a non-priest is liable to death One who removes the ashes the seven sprinklings that are inside or of the leper; and one who raises up onto the altar, whether it is proper or disqualified
Why did hold lotteries? Why did they hold lotteries? as we said Rather, Why did they hold a lottery, and once again hold another lottery,
Rabbi Yoḥanan said: in order to create a commotion throughout courtyard, as it is stated: “We took sweet counsel together, in the House of God we walked with the throng”
With what they held the lottery? Rav Naḥman said: non-sacred garments. And Rav Sheshet said: sacred garments.

Rav Naḥman said: non-sacred garments, because if you say sacred garments, there are armed men who might act with force and perform the service Rav Sheshet said: sacred garments, as, if you say non-sacred garments, due to beloved it may happen they would perform the service
Rav Naḥman said: From where do I state As we learned they gave over to the attendants, and they would take their clothes off them and they would leave only trousers on them.
